title: rbac — HTTP middleware

middleware.description: The primary permission gate. Reads `#[RequirePermission('permission.name')]` from the target action (or accepts `permission:permission.name` inline via the route definition), then calls `PermissionResolver::resolve()`. On false: 403 PermissionDefaultDeny. On true: pass through. This is the middleware every gated route uses.
notes: Reads the #[RequirePermission] attribute from either the controller method OR the action class (Actions convention).
When multiple permissions are declared (#[RequirePermissionAny] or #[RequirePermissionAll]), evaluates the union / intersection accordingly.
Records the check outcome for the audit stream when config('rbac.telemetry.permission_check_sample_ratio') sampled.

middleware.description: Role-based gate. Reads `#[RequireRole('role_name')]` and asserts the caller holds the role. Coarser than permission checks — useful for admin-console routes where 'owner OR admin' is the natural gate (rather than enumerating every permission the role holds).
notes: When both role AND permission attributes are present on a route, both are enforced (AND, not OR).
Skipped for platform_admin guard callers with super_admin role (Gate::before hook).

middleware.description: Ensures the caller's ResolvedPermissionSet is loaded into the process-local cache before the first permission check fires. Skipped when config('rbac.cache.warmup_on_login')=false OR when the request has no authenticated user.

notes: The framework-tier attributes `#[RequirePermission]`, `#[RequireRole]`, `#[RequirePermissionAny]`, `#[RequirePermissionAll]` are DECLARED in `backend-packages/authorization/`. They are READ by `rbac.enforce_permission` / `rbac.enforce_role` middleware. Domain modules decorate their controllers / actions with the attributes and don't reference this module directly.

In routes.json for a domain module, the alias is used inline: `middleware: ['permission:invoices.viewAny']`. This is sugar for `rbac.enforce_permission` reading the explicit permission name instead of the attribute.
